evt_022.label: Drop
evt_022.basis: Party C dropped because it failed to submit or reiterate a proposal by the September 18 final-indication deadline and the filing states that it gave no reason.
evt_022.source_text: Party C did not submit a revised indication of interest or reiterate its prior indication of interest of an all-cash purchase price of $16.00 to $17.00 per share nor did it specify any reasons in connection therewith

initiation_judgment.basis: The Board started the process by authorizing management and BofA Merrill Lynch to explore strategic transactions before any bidder approach appears in the timeline.
initiation_judgment.source_text: authorized Mac-Gray management to work with BofA Merrill Lynch to explore transactional opportunities that might be available to Mac-Gray

advisor_bofa_merrill_lynch.source_text: On May 31, 2013, Mac-Gray entered into an engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch with respect to the Board's undertaking a review of strategic alternatives, including a potential sale process

advisor_goodwin_procter.source_text: Goodwin Procter LLP, outside legal counsel to Mac-Gray

advisor_kirkland.source_text: Kirkland & Ellis LLP, outside legal counsel to CSC/Pamplona

count_reconciliation.assertion: 50 parties were solicited for potential interest in a transaction with Mac-Gray
count_reconciliation.note: The filing counts 50 solicited parties, but the extracted chronology names only four bidder groups: Party A, Party B, Party C, and CSC/Pamplona.
Only Party A and CSC/Pamplona are individually named as strategic bidder groups; the remaining strategic parties are unnamed in the filing.
Only Party B and Party C are individually named as financial bidder groups in the extracted chronology; the remaining financial buyers are unnamed.

count_reconciliation.assertion: 20 potential bidders entered confidentiality agreements with Mac-Gray
count_reconciliation.note: The extracted chronology identifies four named NDA counterparties or bidder groups: Party A, Party B, Party C, and CSC/Pamplona; the remaining NDA counterparties are unnamed.

count_reconciliation.assertion: 2 strategic bidders entered confidentiality agreements with Mac-Gray
count_reconciliation.note: Counts match exactly: the named strategic NDA counterparties are Party A and CSC/Pamplona.

count_reconciliation.assertion: 18 financial bidders entered confidentiality agreements with Mac-Gray
count_reconciliation.note: Only Party B and Party C are individually named as financial NDA counterparties; the remaining financial bidders are unnamed in the filing.

count_reconciliation.assertion: 3 nationally recognized investment banks were invited to submit proposals
count_reconciliation.note: The filing says three investment banks were invited, but only BofA Merrill Lynch is later individually identified; the other invited banks remain unnamed.
